title: chupalon capitatum
intro: Chupalon capitatum, also known as Mexican tea or yerba de pasmo, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family. Traditionally, Chupalon capitatum has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to provide various health benefits, such as relieving headaches, fever, and digestive issues, as well as supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
